On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuppansha}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider appeal while maintaining educational integrity.

The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider appeal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shuppansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shuppansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shuppansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuppansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuppansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-approved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-approved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuppansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider appeal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shuqqansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shuqqansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shuqqansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shuqqansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shuqqansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuqqansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuqqansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-aqqroved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-aqqroved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shuqqansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider aqqeal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />

revision.text:
{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox publisher2 | 外観またはロゴ = [[File:Yamakawa shuppansha.jpg|200px]] | 正式名称 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文名称 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | 前身 = | 現況 = 事業継続中 | 種類 = 株式会社 | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 出版者記号 = 634 | 取次会社 = | 取次コード = 8515 | 設立日 = [[1948年]][[8月25日]] | 代表者 = 代表取締役社長 [[野澤武史]] | 本社郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = [[東京都]][[千代田区]][[内神田]]1−13−13 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 売上高 = | 従業員数 = 42名 ※2019年（[[令和]]元年）9月現在 | 決算期 = | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | ネット販売 = 自社サイト、[[オンライン書店]] | 主要出版物 = 文部科学省検定教科書、学習参考書、問題集（地理歴史科・公民科）「世界歴史大系」、「世界各国史」、「宗教の世界史」、「世界史・日本史リブレット」、「日本歴史大系」、「新体系日本史」、「県史（全国版）」、「歴史散歩（全国版）」、「日本史リブレット"人"」、「日本史広辞典」、「日本史・世界史小辞典」、「日本思想史辞典」、「もういちど読む」シリーズ | 定期刊行物 = | 電子書籍 = | 出版以外の事業 = Webメディア「ヒストリスト」、オーディオブック（音声教科書）、スマートフォンアプリ、タブレットアプリ、電子書籍、CD・DVD・PCソフト・ICT関連商品の制作・販売。 | 得意ジャンル = 歴史、教育、政治、経済、哲学、芸術 | 外部リンク = {{Official URL}} | Twitterurl = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub | 特記事項 = }} {{基礎情報 会社 | 社名 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文社名 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | ロゴ = | 種類 = [[株式会社]] | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 略称 = | 国籍 = {{JPN}} | 郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = 東京都千代田区内神田一丁目13番13号 | 業種 = 5250 | 統一金融機関コード = | SWIFTコード = | 事業内容 = 歴史学関連書籍の出版他 | 代表者 = 代表取締役 野澤武史 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 発行済株式総数 = |売上高 = |営業利益 = |経常利益 = |純利益 = 564万4,000円
（2024年8月期）[https://catr.jp/companies/f125b/65769/settlements/35181/389127 株式会社山川出版社 第81期決算公告] |純資産 = |総資産 = 27億9,779万円
（2024年8月期） | 従業員数 = | 決算期 = 8月期 | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | 関係する人物 = | 外部リンク = | 特記事項 = }} '''株式会社山川出版社'''（やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ、{{lang-en|Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd.}}）は、[[高等学校|高校]][[教科書]]（地歴公民科）・[[参考書]]・[[歴史]]関係の一般書などを刊行する日本の[[出版社]]である。 == 概要 == 1948年（昭和23年）8月、野澤繁二（1914年12月12日-1994年、東京生まれ。[[慶應義塾大学]]経済学部卒）が創業{{Cite news|title=ご挨拶 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 2022年現在の社長は、元[[ラグビーユニオン|ラグビー]]選手の[[野澤武史]]。 専門書から一般書まで幅広く手がけている[[歴史書]]の分野でも老舗である。2009年時点では、教科書以外は40～60代の読者層を想定していたが、2018年には20～30代の社会人向けに「日本史」と「世界史」の「学び直し」をテーマに低価格でカラーページやコラムを大幅に増やした改訂版を出した{{Cite news|title=社会人、副教材で学び直し　政治や科学…時事ネタ満載｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。2018年には創業70周年を迎え、「歴史の転換期」シリーズの刊行を開始した{{Cite news|title=混迷期こそ過去の転機に学ぶ　山川、常識破りの歴史書｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。 2019年11月1日、山川図書出版(株) （東京都新宿区北町34）を吸収合併した。 地理学分野に強い[[二宮書店]]とは2016年から業務提携関係にあり、2025年4月1日をもって二宮書店と経営統合し、新たに'''山川出版社'''となった[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/wp-content/uploads/20241011.pdf 株式会社二宮書店と株式会社山川出版社の統合に関するご案内]。 == 教科書・教材 == *[[文部科学省検定済教科書]]の発行者番号は81。 *高等学校向け検定教科書の[[地理歴史]]科・[[公民 (教科)|公民]]科の各科目（地理を除く）を発行している。 **日本史は、2011年度教科書採択では「日本史A」（31.6%）・「日本史B」（67.8%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **世界史も、2011年度教科書採択では「世界史A」（30.1%）・「世界史B」（56.0%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **同社における「日本史」「世界史」は、1951年（昭和26年）に[[文部省]]（現[[文部科学省]]）検定教科書として採用されて以来、戦後の[[社会 (教科)|社会科]]時代から現在に至るまで歴史教育の基盤となってきた。採択されていない進学校の生徒は各自で買い求めることも多く、実際の市場占有率はさらに高いと考えられている。 *生徒向けの各教科の用語集や一問一答をはじめとする参考書や問題集{{Cite news|title=受験生・高校生 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/student|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}、先生向けのPCソフトやムービーなどの[[DVD]]教材、オーディオブック等も販売している{{Cite news|title=先生方 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 *[[任天堂]]が2004年に開発した携帯型ゲーム機、[[ニンテンドーDS]]に対しては2007年に「山川出版社監修 詳説日本史B 総合トレーニング」と同「世界史B」、2010年にはその改訂版となる「新・総合トレーニングPLUS」を日本史Bと世界史Bの両方で製作・監修し、現在は[[バンダイナムコエンターテインメント]]より発売している[http://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/ バンダイナムコエンターテインメント社サイト内商品紹介]。 == 一般向け書籍 == 一般読者向けに高校[[教科用図書|教科書]]を再編集した「もういちど読むシリーズ」が有名である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=もういちど読むシリーズ|accessdate=2018-09-01}}。 2017年7月時点で累計130万部を突破する[[ベストセラー|ロングセラー]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=こんなに面白い＆役に立つ！　教科書、なぜ大人の間で大ブーム？|accessdate=2018-09-25|website=ビジネスジャーナル/Business Journal {{!}} ビジネスの本音に迫る|language=ja-JP}}。「もういちど読むシリーズ」は現行の教科書の1つ前の版を再構成し、表紙とレイアウトを変更した上で{{Cite web|和書|url=http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/report/20140930/271893/|title=『もういちど読む』を、改めて読む 始まりは「もったいない」＠「日本史の山川」本社|publisher=日経ビジネスONLINE|date=2014-10-16|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018123132/http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/report/20140930/271893/? |archive-date=2014-10-18 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、地図や資料を加えたり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/hit/20100120/1030824/|title=山川出版社の『世界史』『日本史』がヒット中|publisher=日経トレンディネット|date=2010-01-26|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128091109/http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/hit/20100120/1030824/ |archive-date=2010-01-28 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、注釈を本文中に組み込み太字装飾も解除するなど、一般向けに読みやすく変更されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://toyokeizai.net/articles/-/74254?page=2|title=大人の教養は｢教科書｣で身につけよう！下手なハウツー本よりずっと使える (p.2)|publisher=東洋経済ONLINE|date=2015-06-23|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。 2009年8月30日に刊行を開始した「もういちど読む山川日本史」「もういちど読む山川世界史」は幅広い客層に受け入れられ異例のヒットとなった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-cast.com/2010/02/14059734.html?p=all|title=もう一度読む「山川歴史教科書」「歴女」や若手ビジネスマンに人気|publisher=J-CASTニュース|date=2010-02-14|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。これを受け、[[数研出版]]とコラボレーションして同シリーズを展開している。 歴史学の必携書と評価の高い書籍も多数展開している。「世界歴史大系」「世界各国史」「宗教の世界史」「世界史・日本史リブレット」「日本歴史大系」「新体系日本史」「県史（全国版）」「歴史散歩（全国版）」「日本史リブレット"人"」をはじめとする多くのシリーズがあり、辞典分野では、20世紀の日本史学を統括する「日本史広辞典」をはじめ、「日本史・世界史小辞典」「日本思想史辞典」を刊行している。 研究学術書の分野でも、[[騎馬民族征服王朝説]]を提唱した[[江上波夫]]の文化史論集や、近代ドイツ史の研究者として後に[[東京大学]]の総長も務めた[[林健太郎 (歴史学者)|林健太郎]]の著作集など歴史学界に大きな足跡を残す多くの論集を刊行。[[1889年]]創刊の雑誌『[[史学雑誌|史學雜誌]]』も取り扱っている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ 山川出版社] * {{Facebook|YamakawaShuppansha}} * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * {{Instagram|yamakawashuppansha}} * {{YouTube|channel = UCzOTYuiZPWLnh2pWEtNU3wg}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ ヒストリスト［Historist］−歴史と教科書の山川出版社の情報メディア−] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかわしゆつはんしや}} [[Category:日本の出版社]] [[Category:日本史に関する出版社]] [[Category:千代田区の企業]] [[Category:1948年設立の企業]] [[Category:教科書出版社]] [[Category:神田]]

{{Infobox publisher2 | 外観またはロゴ = [[File:Yamakawa shuppansha.jpg|200px]] | 正式名称 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文名称 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | 前身 = | 現況 = 事業継続中 | 種類 = 株式会社 | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 出版者記号 = 634 | 取次会社 = | 取次コード = 8515 | 設立日 = [[1948年]][[8月25日]] | 代表者 = 代表取締役社長 [[野澤武史]] | 本社郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = [[東京都]][[千代田区]][[内神田]]1−13−13 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 売上高 = | 従業員数 = 42名 ※2019年（[[令和]]元年）9月現在 | 決算期 = | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | ネット販売 = 自社サイト、[[オンライン書店]] | 主要出版物 = 文部科学省検定教科書、学習参考書、問題集（地理歴史科・公民科）「世界歴史大系」、「世界各国史」、「宗教の世界史」、「世界史・日本史リブレット」、「日本歴史大系」、「新体系日本史」、「県史（全国版）」、「歴史散歩（全国版）」、「日本史リブレット"人"」、「日本史広辞典」、「日本史・世界史小辞典」、「日本思想史辞典」、「もういちど読む」シリーズ | 定期刊行物 = | 電子書籍 = | 出版以外の事業 = Webメディア「ヒストリスト」、オーディオブック（音声教科書）、スマートフォンアプリ、タブレットアプリ、電子書籍、CD・DVD・PCソフト・ICT関連商品の制作・販売。 | 得意ジャンル = 歴史、教育、政治、経済、哲学、芸術 | 外部リンク = {{Official URL}} | Twitterurl = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub | 特記事項 = }} {{基礎情報 会社 | 社名 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文社名 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | ロゴ = | 種類 = [[株式会社]] | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 略称 = | 国籍 = {{JPN}} | 郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = 東京都千代田区内神田一丁目13番13号 | 業種 = 5250 | 統一金融機関コード = | SWIFTコード = | 事業内容 = 歴史学関連書籍の出版他 | 代表者 = 代表取締役 野澤武史 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 発行済株式総数 = |売上高 = |営業利益 = |経常利益 = |純利益 = 564万4,000円
（2024年8月期）[https://catr.jp/companies/f125b/65769/settlements/35181/389127 株式会社山川出版社 第81期決算公告] |純資産 = |総資産 = 27億9,779万円
（2024年8月期） | 従業員数 = | 決算期 = 8月期 | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | 関係する人物 = | 外部リンク = | 特記事項 = }} '''株式会社山川出版社'''（やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ、{{lang-en|Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd.}}）は、[[高等学校|高校]][[教科書]]（地歴公民科）・[[参考書]]・[[歴史]]関係の一般書などを刊行する日本の[[出版社]]である。 == 概要 == 1948年（昭和23年）8月、野澤繁二（1914年12月12日-1994年、東京生まれ。[[慶應義塾大学]]経済学部卒）が創業{{Cite news|title=ご挨拶 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 2022年現在の社長は、元[[ラグビーユニオン|ラグビー]]選手の[[野澤武史]]。 専門書から一般書まで幅広く手がけている[[歴史書]]の分野でも老舗である。2009年時点では、教科書以外は40～60代の読者層を想定していたが、2018年には20～30代の社会人向けに「日本史」と「世界史」の「学び直し」をテーマに低価格でカラーページやコラムを大幅に増やした改訂版を出した{{Cite news|title=社会人、副教材で学び直し　政治や科学…時事ネタ満載｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。2018年には創業70周年を迎え、「歴史の転換期」シリーズの刊行を開始した{{Cite news|title=混迷期こそ過去の転機に学ぶ　山川、常識破りの歴史書｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。 2019年11月1日、山川図書出版(株) （東京都新宿区北町34）を吸収合併した。 地理学分野に強い[[二宮書店]]とは2016年から業務提携関係にあり、2025年4月1日をもって二宮書店と経営統合し、新たに'''山川出版社'''となった[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/wp-content/uploads/20241011.pdf 株式会社二宮書店と株式会社山川出版社の統合に関するご案内]。 == 教科書・教材 == *[[文部科学省検定済教科書]]の発行者番号は81。 *高等学校向け検定教科書の[[地理歴史]]科・[[公民 (教科)|公民]]科の各科目（地理は、令和7年に吸収した[[二宮書店]]発行分を継承したものとなり、令和8年度入学者分から学年進行で本格参入予定）を発行している。 **日本史は、2011年度教科書採択では「日本史A」（31.6%）・「日本史B」（67.8%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **世界史も、2011年度教科書採択では「世界史A」（30.1%）・「世界史B」（56.0%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **同社における「日本史」「世界史」は、1951年（昭和26年）に[[文部省]]（現[[文部科学省]]）検定教科書として採用されて以来、戦後の[[社会 (教科)|社会科]]時代から現在に至るまで歴史教育の基盤となってきた。採択されていない進学校の生徒は各自で買い求めることも多く、実際の市場占有率はさらに高いと考えられている。 *生徒向けの各教科の用語集や一問一答をはじめとする参考書や問題集{{Cite news|title=受験生・高校生 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/student|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}、先生向けのPCソフトやムービーなどの[[DVD]]教材、オーディオブック等も販売している{{Cite news|title=先生方 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 *[[任天堂]]が2004年に開発した携帯型ゲーム機、[[ニンテンドーDS]]に対しては2007年に「山川出版社監修 詳説日本史B 総合トレーニング」と同「世界史B」、2010年にはその改訂版となる「新・総合トレーニングPLUS」を日本史Bと世界史Bの両方で製作・監修し、現在は[[バンダイナムコエンターテインメント]]より発売している[http://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/ バンダイナムコエンターテインメント社サイト内商品紹介]。 == 一般向け書籍 == 一般読者向けに高校[[教科用図書|教科書]]を再編集した「もういちど読むシリーズ」が有名である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=もういちど読むシリーズ|accessdate=2018-09-01}}。 2017年7月時点で累計130万部を突破する[[ベストセラー|ロングセラー]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=こんなに面白い＆役に立つ！　教科書、なぜ大人の間で大ブーム？|accessdate=2018-09-25|website=ビジネスジャーナル/Business Journal {{!}} ビジネスの本音に迫る|language=ja-JP}}。「もういちど読むシリーズ」は現行の教科書の1つ前の版を再構成し、表紙とレイアウトを変更した上で{{Cite web|和書|url=http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/report/20140930/271893/|title=『もういちど読む』を、改めて読む 始まりは「もったいない」＠「日本史の山川」本社|publisher=日経ビジネスONLINE|date=2014-10-16|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018123132/http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/report/20140930/271893/? |archive-date=2014-10-18 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、地図や資料を加えたり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/hit/20100120/1030824/|title=山川出版社の『世界史』『日本史』がヒット中|publisher=日経トレンディネット|date=2010-01-26|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128091109/http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/hit/20100120/1030824/ |archive-date=2010-01-28 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、注釈を本文中に組み込み太字装飾も解除するなど、一般向けに読みやすく変更されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://toyokeizai.net/articles/-/74254?page=2|title=大人の教養は｢教科書｣で身につけよう！下手なハウツー本よりずっと使える (p.2)|publisher=東洋経済ONLINE|date=2015-06-23|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。 2009年8月30日に刊行を開始した「もういちど読む山川日本史」「もういちど読む山川世界史」は幅広い客層に受け入れられ異例のヒットとなった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-cast.com/2010/02/14059734.html?p=all|title=もう一度読む「山川歴史教科書」「歴女」や若手ビジネスマンに人気|publisher=J-CASTニュース|date=2010-02-14|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。これを受け、[[数研出版]]とコラボレーションして同シリーズを展開している。 歴史学の必携書と評価の高い書籍も多数展開している。「世界歴史大系」「世界各国史」「宗教の世界史」「世界史・日本史リブレット」「日本歴史大系」「新体系日本史」「県史（全国版）」「歴史散歩（全国版）」「日本史リブレット"人"」をはじめとする多くのシリーズがあり、辞典分野では、20世紀の日本史学を統括する「日本史広辞典」をはじめ、「日本史・世界史小辞典」「日本思想史辞典」を刊行している。 研究学術書の分野でも、[[騎馬民族征服王朝説]]を提唱した[[江上波夫]]の文化史論集や、近代ドイツ史の研究者として後に[[東京大学]]の総長も務めた[[林健太郎 (歴史学者)|林健太郎]]の著作集など歴史学界に大きな足跡を残す多くの論集を刊行。[[1889年]]創刊の雑誌『[[史学雑誌|史學雜誌]]』も取り扱っている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ 山川出版社] * {{Facebook|YamakawaShuppansha}} * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * {{Instagram|yamakawashuppansha}} * {{YouTube|channel = UCzOTYuiZPWLnh2pWEtNU3wg}} * 専門書から一般書まで幅広く手がけている[[歴史書]]の分野でも老舗である。2009年時点では、教科書以外は40～60代の読者層を想定していたが、2018年には20～30代の社会人向けに「日本史」と「世界史」の「学び直し」をテーマに低価格でカラーページやコラムを大幅に増やした改訂版を出した{{Cite news|title=社会人、副教材で学び直し　政治や科学…時事ネタ満載｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。2018年には創業70周年を迎え、「歴史の転換期」シリーズの刊行を開始した{{Cite news|title=混迷期こそ過去の転機に学ぶ　山川、常識破りの歴史書｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。 2019年11月1日、山川図書出版(株) （東京都新宿区北町34）を吸収合併した。 地理学分野に強い[[二宮書店]]とは2016年から業務提携関係にあり、2025年4月1日をもって二宮書店と経営統合し、新たに'''山川出版社'''となった[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/wp-content/uploads/20241011.pdf 株式会社二宮書店と株式会社山川出版社の統合に関するご案内]。 == 教科書・教材 == *[[文部科学省検定済教科書]]の発行者番号は81。 *高等学校向け検定教科書の[[地理歴史]]科・[[公民 (教科)|公民]]科の各科目（地理は、令和7年に吸収した[[二宮書店]]発行分を継承したものとなり、令和8年度入学者分から学年進行で本格参入予定）を発行している。 **日本史は、2011年度教科書採択では「日本史A」（31.6%）・「日本史B」（67.8%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **世界史も、2011年度教科書採択では「世界史A」（30.1%）・「世界史B」（56.0%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **同社における「日本史」「世界史」は、1951年（昭和26年）に[[文部省]]（現[[文部科学省]]）検定教科書として採用されて以来、戦後の[[社会 (教科)|社会科]]時代から現在に至るまで歴史教育の基盤となってきた。採択されていない進学校の生徒は各自で買い求めることも多く、実際の市場占有率はさらに高いと考えられている。 *生徒向けの各教科の用語集や一問一答をはじめとする参考書や問題集{{Cite news|title=受験生・高校生 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/student|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}、先生向けのPCソフトやムービーなどの[[DVD]]教材、オーディオブック等も販売している{{Cite news|title=先生方 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 *[[任天堂]]が2004年に開発した携帯型ゲーム機、[[ニンテンドーDS]]に対しては2007年に「山川出版社監修 詳説日本史B 総合トレーニング」と同「世界史B」、2010年にはその改訂版となる「新・総合トレーニングPLUS」を日本史Bと世界史Bの両方で製作・監修し、現在は[[バンダイナムコエンターテインメント]]より発売している[http://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/ バンダイナムコエンターテインメント社サイト内商品紹介]。 == 一般向け書籍 == 一般読者向けに高校[[教科用図書|教科書]]を再編集した「もういちど読むシリーズ」が有名である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=もういちど読むシリーズ|accessdate=2018-09-01}}。 2017年7月時点で累計130万部を突破する[[ベストセラー|ロングセラー]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=こんなに面白い＆役に立つ！　教科書、なぜ大人の間で大ブーム？|accessdate=2018-09-25|website=ビジネスジャーナル/Business Journal {{!}} ビジネスの本音に迫る|language=ja-JP}}。「もういちど読むシリーズ」は現行の教科書の1つ前の版を再構成し、表紙とレイアウトを変更した上で{{Cite web|和書|url=http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/report/20140930/271893/|title=『もういちど読む』を、改めて読む 始まりは「もったいない」＠「日本史の山川」本社|publisher=日経ビジネスONLINE|date=2014-10-16|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018123132/http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/report/20140930/271893/? |archive-date=2014-10-18 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、地図や資料を加えたり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/hit/20100120/1030824/|title=山川出版社の『世界史』『日本史』がヒット中|publisher=日経トレンディネット|date=2010-01-26|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128091109/http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/hit/20100120/1030824/ |archive-date=2010-01-28 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、注釈を本文中に組み込み太字装飾も解除するなど、一般向けに読みやすく変更されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://toyokeizai.net/articles/-/74254?page=2|title=大人の教養は｢教科書｣で身につけよう！下手なハウツー本よりずっと使える (p.2)|publisher=東洋経済ONLINE|date=2015-06-23|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。 2009年8月30日に刊行を開始した「もういちど読む山川日本史」「もういちど読む山川世界史」は幅広い客層に受け入れられ異例のヒットとなった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.j-cast.com/2010/02/14059734.html?p=all|title=もう一度読む「山川歴史教科書」「歴女」や若手ビジネスマンに人気|publisher=J-CASTニュース|date=2010-02-14|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。これを受け、[[数研出版]]とコラボレーションして同シリーズを展開している。 歴史学の必携書と評価の高い書籍も多数展開している。「世界歴史大系」「世界各国史」「宗教の世界史」「世界史・日本史リブレット」「日本歴史大系」「新体系日本史」「県史（全国版）」「歴史散歩（全国版）」「日本史リブレット"人"」をはじめとする多くのシリーズがあり、辞典分野では、20世紀の日本史学を統括する「日本史広辞典」をはじめ、「日本史・世界史小辞典」「日本思想史辞典」を刊行している。 研究学術書の分野でも、[[騎馬民族征服王朝説]]を提唱した[[江上波夫]]の文化史論集や、近代ドイツ史の研究者として後に[[東京大学]]の総長も務めた[[林健太郎 (歴史学者)|林健太郎]]の著作集など歴史学界に大きな足跡を残す多くの論集を刊行。[[1889年]]創刊の雑誌『[[史学雑誌|史學雜誌]]』も取り扱っている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ 山川出版社] * {{Facebook|YamakawaShuppansha}} * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * {{Instagram|yamakawashuppansha}} * {{YouTube|channel = UCzOTYuiZPWLnh2pWEtNU3wg}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ ヒストリスト［Historist］−歴史と教科書の山川出版社の情報メディア−] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかわしゆつはんしや}} [[Category:日本の出版社]] [[Category:日本史に関する出版社]] [[Category:千代田区の企業]] [[Category:1948年設立の企業]] [[Category:教科書出版社]] [[Category:神田]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[山川出版社]] to [[Yamakawa Shuppansha]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox publisher2 | 外観またはロゴ = [[File:Yamakawa shuppansha.jpg|200px]] | 正式名称 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文名称 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | 前身 = | 現況 = 事業継続中 | 種類 = 株式会社 | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 出版者記号 = 634 | 取次会社 = | 取次コード = 8515 | 設立日 = [[1948年]][[8月25日]] | 代表者 = 代表取締役社長 [[野澤武史]] | 本社郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = [[東京都]][[千代田区]][[内神田]]1−13−13 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 売上高 = | 従業員数 = 42名 ※2019年（[[令和]]元年）9月現在 | 決算期 = | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | ネット販売 = 自社サイト、[[オンライン書店]] | 主要出版物 = 文部科学省検定教科書、学習参考書、問題集（地理歴史科・公民科）「世界歴史大系」、「世界各国史」、「宗教の世界史」、「世界史・日本史リブレット」、「日本歴史大系」、「新体系日本史」、「県史（全国版）」、「歴史散歩（全国版）」、「日本史リブレット"人"」、「日本史広辞典」、「日本史・世界史小辞典」、「日本思想史辞典」、「もういちど読む」シリーズ | 定期刊行物 = | 電子書籍 = | 出版以外の事業 = Webメディア「ヒストリスト」、オーディオブック（音声教科書）、スマートフォンアプリ、タブレットアプリ、電子書籍、CD・DVD・PCソフト・ICT関連商品の制作・販売。 | 得意ジャンル = 歴史、教育、政治、経済、哲学、芸術 | 外部リンク = {{Official URL}} | Twitterurl = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub | 特記事項 = }} {{基礎情報 会社 | 社名 = 株式会社山川出版社 | 英文社名 = Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd. | ロゴ = | 種類 = [[株式会社]] | 市場情報 = 非上場 | 略称 = | 国籍 = {{JPN}} | 郵便番号 = 101-0047 | 本社所在地 = 東京都千代田区内神田一丁目13番13号 | 業種 = 5250 | 統一金融機関コード = | SWIFTコード = | 事業内容 = 歴史学関連書籍の出版他 | 代表者 = 代表取締役 野澤武史 | 資本金 = 1,050万円 | 発行済株式総数 = |売上高 = |営業利益 = |経常利益 = |純利益 = 564万4,000円
（2024年8月期）[https://catr.jp/companies/f125b/65769/settlements/35181/389127 株式会社山川出版社 第81期決算公告] |純資産 = |総資産 = 27億9,779万円
（2024年8月期） | 従業員数 = | 決算期 = 8月期 | 主要株主 = | 主要子会社 = | 関係する人物 = | 外部リンク = | 特記事項 = }} '''株式会社山川出版社'''（やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ、{{lang-en|Yamakawa Shuppansha Ltd.}}）は、[[高等学校|高校]][[教科書]]（地歴公民科）・[[参考書]]・[[歴史]]関係の一般書などを刊行する日本の[[出版社]]である。 == 概要 == 1948年（昭和23年）8月、野澤繁二（1914年12月12日-1994年、東京生まれ。[[慶應義塾大学]]経済学部卒）が創業{{Cite news|title=ご挨拶 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 2022年現在の社長は、元[[ラグビーユニオン|ラグビー]]選手の[[野澤武史]]。 専門書から一般書まで幅広く手がけている[[歴史書]]の分野でも老舗である。2009年時点では、教科書以外は40～60代の読者層を想定していたが、2018年には20～30代の社会人向けに「日本史」と「世界史」の「学び直し」をテーマに低価格でカラーページやコラムを大幅に増やした改訂版を出した{{Cite news|title=社会人、副教材で学び直し　政治や科学…時事ネタ満載｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。2018年には創業70周年を迎え、「歴史の転換期」シリーズの刊行を開始した{{Cite news|title=混迷期こそ過去の転機に学ぶ　山川、常識破りの歴史書｜エンタメ！｜NIKKEI STYLE|last=日本経済新聞社・日経BP社|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000/|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=NIKKEI STYLE}}。 2019年11月1日、山川図書出版(株) （東京都新宿区北町34）を吸収合併した。 地理学分野に強い[[二宮書店]]とは2016年から業務提携関係にあり、2025年4月1日をもって二宮書店と経営統合し、新たに'''山川出版社'''となった[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/wp-content/uploads/20241011.pdf 株式会社二宮書店と株式会社山川出版社の統合に関するご案内]。 == 教科書・教材 == *[[文部科学省検定済教科書]]の発行者番号は81。 *高等学校向け検定教科書の[[地理歴史]]科・[[公民 (教科)|公民]]科の各科目（地理は、令和7年に吸収した[[二宮書店]]発行分を継承したものとなり、令和8年度入学者分から学年進行で本格参入予定）を発行している。 **日本史は、2011年度教科書採択では「日本史A」（31.6%）・「日本史B」（67.8%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **世界史も、2011年度教科書採択では「世界史A」（30.1%）・「世界史B」（56.0%）とも発行者別占有率は第1位である。 **同社における「日本史」「世界史」は、1951年（昭和26年）に[[文部省]]（現[[文部科学省]]）検定教科書として採用されて以来、戦後の[[社会 (教科)|社会科]]時代から現在に至るまで歴史教育の基盤となってきた。採択されていない進学校の生徒は各自で買い求めることも多く、実際の市場占有率はさらに高いと考えられている。 *生徒向けの各教科の用語集や一問一答をはじめとする参考書や問題集{{Cite news|title=受験生・高校生 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/student|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}、先生向けのPCソフトやムービーなどの[[DVD]]教材、オーディオブック等も販売している{{Cite news|title=先生方 {{!}} 山川出版社|last=山川出版社|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2018-09-25|language=ja|work=歴史と教科書の山川出版社}}。 *[[任天堂]]が2004年に開発した携帯型ゲーム機、[[ニンテンドーDS]]に対しては2007年に「山川出版社監修 詳説日本史B 総合トレーニング」と同「世界史B」、2010年にはその改訂版となる「新・総合トレーニングPLUS」を日本史Bと世界史Bの両方で製作・監修し、現在は[[バンダイナムコエンターテインメント]]より発売している[http://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/ バンダイナムコエンターテインメント社サイト内商品紹介]。 == 一般向け書籍 == 一般読者向けに高校[[教科用図書|教科書]]を再編集した「もういちど読むシリーズ」が有名である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=もういちど読むシリーズ|accessdate=2018-09-01}}。 2017年7月時点で累計130万部を突破する[[ベストセラー|ロングセラー]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=こんなに面白い＆役に立つ！　教科書、なぜ大人の間で大ブーム？|accessdate=2018-09-25|website=ビジネスジャーナル/Business Journal {{!}} ビジネスの本音に迫る|language=ja-JP}}。「もういちど読むシリーズ」は現行の教科書の1つ前の版を再構成し、表紙とレイアウトを変更した上で{{Cite web|和書|url=http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/report/20140930/271893/|title=『もういちど読む』を、改めて読む 始まりは「もったいない」＠「日本史の山川」本社|publisher=日経ビジネスONLINE|date=2014-10-16|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018123132/http://business.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/report/20140930/271893/? |archive-date=2014-10-18 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、地図や資料を加えたり{{Cite web|和書|url=http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp/article/hit/20100120/1030824/|title=山川出版社の『世界史』『日本史』がヒット中|publisher=日経トレンディネット|date=2010-01-26|accessdate=2016-03-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100128091109/http://trendy.nikkeibp.co.jp:80/article/hit/20100120/1030824/ |archive-date=2010-01-28 |url-status=dead |url-status-date=2024-09-23}}、注釈を本文中に組み込み太字装飾も解除するなど、一般向けに読みやすく変更されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://toyokeizai.net/articles/-/74254?page=2|title=大人の教養は｢教科書｣で身につけよう！下手なハウツー本よりずっと使える (p.2)|publisher=東洋経済ONLINE|date=2015-06-23|accessdate=2016-03-01}}。 2009年8月30日に刊行を開始した「もういちど読む山川日本史」「もういちど読む山川世界史」は幅広い客層に受け入れられ異例のヒットとなった{{Cite 研究学術書の分野でも、[[騎馬民族征服王朝説]]を提唱した[[江上波夫]]の文化史論集や、近代ドイツ史の研究者として後に[[東京大学]]の総長も務めた[[林健太郎 (歴史学者)|林健太郎]]の著作集など歴史学界に大きな足跡を残す多くの論集を刊行。[[1889年]]創刊の雑誌『[[史学雑誌|史學雜誌]]』も取り扱っている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ 山川出版社] * {{Facebook|YamakawaShuppansha}} * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * {{Instagram|yamakawashuppansha}} * {{YouTube|channel = UCzOTYuiZPWLnh2pWEtNU3wg}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ ヒストリスト［Historist］−歴史と教科書の山川出版社の情報メディア−] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかわしゆつはんしや}} [[Category:日本の出版社]] [[Category:日本史に関する出版社]] [[Category:千代田区の企業]] [[Category:1948年設立の企業]] [[Category:教科書出版社]] [[Category:神田]]

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]
{{translated page|ja|山川出版社|version=104638750|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

[[sn:Yamakawa Shuppansha]] [[sn:Yamakawa Shu11ansha]] [[ja:山川出版社]] [[sn:amakawa Shu11ansha]] [[Category:Companies established in 1948]] [[Category:千代田区の企業]] [[Category:教科書出版社]] [[Category:Publishing companies of Japan]] [[Category:日本史に関する出版社]]

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Nozawa|qq=}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />

{{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Nozawa|qq=}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]
[[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|山川出版社|version=104638750|comment=Imported full ja history}}

By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山川出版社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11468274]] Wikidata name: Yamakawa Shuppansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Nozawa|1=Takeshi Nozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690584}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山川出版社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11468274]] Wikidata name: Yamakawa Shuppansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox publisher2 | image = [[File:Yamakawa shu11ansha.jpg|200px]] | name = Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd. | established = [[August 25, 1948]] | location_city = [[Chiyoda, Tokyo]] | location_country = Japan | key_people = {{ill|Takeshi Nozawa|ja|野澤武史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Nozawa|1=Takeshi Nozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28690584}}, Representative Director | revenue = | employees = 42 (as of September 2019) | website = {{Official URL|https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/}} | twitter = https://twitter.com/yamakawapub }}
'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
== References == {{Footnotes}} <references />
== External Links == * [https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/ Yamakawa Shu11ansha Official Website] * {{Twitter|yamakawapub}} * [https://www.historist.jp/ Historist - History and Textbooks by Yamakawa]

== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."

'''Yamakawa Shu11ansha Ltd.''' (株式会社山川出版社, やまかわしゅっぱんしゃ) is a Japanese publishing company specializing in high school textbooks (history and civics) and reference books related to history.
== Overview == Yamakawa Shu11ansha was founded in August 1948 by Shigenji Nozawa (1914–1994), a graduate of Keio University's Faculty of Economics<ref>{{Cite news|title=Greetings from Yamakawa Shu11ansha|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/company/aisatsu|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. As of 2022, the president is Takeshi Nozawa, a former rugby player.
Known for its comprehensive historical texts, the company has a long-standing reputation in the field. By 2018, Yamakawa had expanded its audience to younger readers in their 20s and 30s by revising its popular "World History" and "Japanese History" series to include more color pages and columns<ref>{{Cite news|title=Revamping History Textbooks for Adults|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXKZO15392140X10C17A4BE0P01|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>. In 2019, the company celebrated its 70th anniversary with the launch of the "Turning Points in History" series<ref>{{Cite news|title=Learning from History in Times of Uncertainty|url=https://style.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO29429060W8A410C1000000|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Nikkei Style}}</ref>.
On November 1, 2019, Yamakawa merged with Yamakawa Tosho Publishing<ref name="merger">{{Cite news|title=Notice of Merger|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/news/merger/|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company has also announced a business integration with Ninomiya Shoten, a leading geography publisher, scheduled for April 2025<ref>[https://www.ninomiyashoten.co.jp/news/integration.pdf Integration Announcement]</ref>.
== Textbooks and Educational Materials == * Yamakawa is registered under publisher code 81 for Ministry of Education-a11roved textbooks. * High school textbooks in history and civics (excluding geography) dominate the market, with significant shares in 2011: 31.6% for "Japanese History A" and 67.8% for "Japanese History B." * The "World History" and "Japanese History" textbooks have been central to postwar education since their adoption as Ministry-a11roved textbooks in 1951. * Additional resources include glossaries, Q&A books, and DVDs for educators<ref>{{Cite news|title=Resources for Teachers|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/teacher|accessdate=2023-01-10|language=ja|work=Yamakawa Shu11ansha}}</ref>. The company also supervises educational games for the Nintendo DS platform<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bandainamcoent.co.jp/cs/list/ds_yamakawa/|title=Educational Games by Yamakawa|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>.
== General Publications == Yamakawa's "One More Read" series repackages high school textbooks for general readers<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yamakawa.co.jp/general/mouichido|title=One More Read Series|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. By 2017, it had sold over 1.3 million copies<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://biz-journal.jp/health/post_24054.html|title=Textbooks for Adults Are a Hit|accessdate=2023-01-10}}</ref>. The series simplifies academic content for wider a11eal while maintaining educational integrity.
The company also publishes scholarly works, including the "World History Series" and "Japanese History Series," which are highly regarded by researchers. Yamakawa's publications are often considered essential for historical studies, including encyclopedic dictionaries like the "Japanese Historical Dictionary."
